experiment: detection locus — Llama-3.2-1B cross-architecture replication on multi-hop transitive-ordering logic: COMPETENCE-FLOOR, infeasible (pre-confirmatory feasibility probe; no hypothesis test run)
measurement: greedy one-shot on all 48 seeded items (24 EASY + 24 HARD), no resampling
RESULT: COMPETENCE_FLOOR — no genuine CORRECT class; cross-architecture logic replication INFEASIBLE on Llama-1B
interpretation: Llama-3.2-1B answers '2' reflexively to ALL 24 easy ordering questions; the 6 'correct' are EXACTLY the 6 items whose true answer is 2 (reflex-match, not reasoning). No competent correct class -> the confab-vs-correct B_contrast comparison is undefined. A forced AUC would separate confident-reflex (the answer-2 items, low entropy) from uncertain-confab (high entropy), NOT competence-correct from confab, so no AUC is reported. Cross-architecture single-pass legibility remains established on ARITHMETIC (Llama-1B AUC 1.00, PREREG_detection_locus_llama); the LOGIC domain's inference-depth difficulty pushes the 1B model below the competence floor.
honest_scope: single second open model Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct; multi-hop transitive-ordering logic; pre-confirmatory feasibility probe only (greedy one-shot, no resampling, no confirmatory AUC run because the CORRECT class cannot be genuinely populated); a competence-floor boundary, NOT a legibility verdict. The Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct logic finding (REPORT_AS_LANDED, 3-domain domain-general) is unaffected.
repro: python papers/grounded-honesty-axis/run_detection_locus_logic.py --model meta-llama/Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct  (per-item greedy v1 in stdout reveals the reflexive '2'; confirmatory not powered)
